name: Diao Chan
description: Diao Chan is a legendary figure in Chinese history and one of the Four Beauties of ancient China. She is best known for her role in Luo Guanzhong's historical novel Romance of the Three Kingdoms, although whether she was a historical person is debated. In the novel, Diao Chan conspires with the official Wang Yun to use her beauty to cause dissension between the tyrannical warlord Dong Zhuo and his adopted son Lü Bu. Wang Yun presents Diao Chan to Dong Zhuo as a concubine, and simultaneously promises her to Lü Bu in marriage. Diao Chan then uses her charm and beauty to turn Dong Zhuo and Lü Bu against each other, eventually leading to Dong Zhuo's assassination by Lü Bu. Her actions are credited with destabilizing Dong Zhuo's reign of terror and contributing to the eventual reunification of China. Diao Chan's story is a tale of sacrifice, courage, and the power of beauty used for strategic purposes. She represents a figure who, despite her seemingly powerless position, was able to manipulate powerful men and influence the course of history, becoming a symbol of female agency and cunning within a patriarchal society. Her legacy continues to be celebrated in opera, theater, and popular culture, solidifying her status as a beloved and enduring figure in Chinese folklore.
subtitle: Beauty that toppled a tyrant
achievements: Celebrated as one of the Four Beauties

keyMyths.title: The Beauty Trap
keyMyths.description: Diao Chan is presented as a concubine to Dong Zhuo by Wang Yun, while secretly promised to Lü Bu. This sets the stage for jealousy and betrayal between the two powerful men.

keyMyths.title: Sowing Discord
keyMyths.description: Diao Chan uses her charm and seductive abilities to turn Dong Zhuo and Lü Bu against each other. She manipulates their emotions, creating a deep rift between them.

keyMyths.title: The Assassination
keyMyths.description: Fueled by jealousy and Diao Chan's manipulations, Lü Bu assassinates Dong Zhuo. This act removes a major threat to the Han Dynasty and paves the way for future conflicts.

sources.author: Luo Guanzhong
sources.description: The primary source for the story of Diao Chan and her role in the downfall of Dong Zhuo.
sources.text: The primary source for the story of Diao Chan and her role in the downfall of Dong Zhuo.
